title: Entada polystachya
intro: Entada polystachya, also known as the giant tree climber, is a tropical legume native to Southeast Asia and Australia, characterized by its unique, long-stemmed pods containing multiple seeds. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Entada polystachya include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Entada polystachya is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a powder or capsule, to aid in the treatment of respiratory and fever-related conditions.
taxonomy: Entada polystachya belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legume or pea family. This classification is based on its characteristic flowers, fruits, and seed pods that resemble those of other Fabaceae species. Within the Fabaceae family, Entada polystachya is further classified as a member of the tribe Faboideae. The genus Entada is also a part of the Fabaceae family, and it is known for its unique and often woody vines. The specific classification of Entada polystachya is typically as follows: genus - Entada, species - E. polystachya, family - Fabaceae.
